title: Add reject-propagation tests for Claude invoke wrappers in bridge tests
analysis: The existing claudeBridge.test.ts has 6 happy-path tests for the 'claude invoke wrappers' describe block (lines 28-116). All use `mockInvoke.mockResolvedValue(undefined)` and verify call arguments. None test rejection propagation. The wrappers in bridge.ts (claudeSendMessage, claudeAbort, claudeClearSession) all use `return invoke(...)`, which naturally propagates rejections. However, a regression (e.g., wrapping invoke in try/catch or adding error swallowing) would silently break the `.catch()` handlers in App.tsx. Three new tests are needed — one per wrapper — following the exact pattern from the task description: `mockInvoke.mockRejectedValue(new Error('IPC failed'))` then `await expect(wrapper()).rejects.toThrow('IPC failed')`. These tests go inside the existing 'claude invoke wrappers' describe block, after the existing happy-path tests (after line 116, before line 117).

steps.description: Add test: claudeSendMessage propagates invoke rejection. Inside the 'claude invoke wrappers' describe block, add a test that calls `mockInvoke.mockRejectedValue(new Error('IPC failed'))`, invokes `claudeSendMessage('hello')`, and asserts `rejects.toThrow('IPC failed')`. Run tests to confirm it passes (this is a regression guard — the wrapper already propagates correctly via `return invoke(...)`).
Add test: claudeAbort propagates invoke rejection. Same pattern: `mockInvoke.mockRejectedValue(new Error('IPC failed'))`, call `claudeAbort()`, assert `rejects.toThrow('IPC failed')`.
Add test: claudeClearSession propagates invoke rejection. Same pattern: `mockInvoke.mockRejectedValue(new Error('IPC failed'))`, call `claudeClearSession()`, assert `rejects.toThrow('IPC failed')`.

design_decisions.decision: Place all three rejection tests inside the existing 'claude invoke wrappers' describe block, after the existing happy-path tests
design_decisions.rationale: The describe block already groups tests for these three wrappers. Adding rejection tests here maintains logical cohesion and follows the file's existing structure.

design_decisions.decision: Use 'IPC failed' as the error message in all three tests
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the task description exactly. The specific message doesn't matter for the regression guard — what matters is that the same Error object propagates unchanged.

design_decisions.decision: Test-only steps with no separate impl steps
design_decisions.rationale: The wrappers already propagate rejections correctly (they use `return invoke(...)`). These are regression-guard tests, not TDD for new behavior. No implementation change is needed.

design_decisions.decision: Three separate steps rather than one combined step
design_decisions.rationale: Each wrapper is an independent unit under test. Separate steps allow atomic commits and make it clear which wrapper each test covers, even though the pattern is identical.

reuse.how: Reuse the existing `const mockInvoke = vi.mocked(invoke)` reference declared at module scope. Use `mockRejectedValue` instead of `mockResolvedValue`.
The listener rollback tests already use `await expect(...).rejects.toThrow(...)`. Reuse the exact same assertion pattern for invoke rejection tests.
The existing `beforeEach(() => vi.clearAllMocks())` ensures each test starts clean. No additional setup needed.
